skill.name: data-validation
skill.description: Guides implementation of data validation for API payloads, form inputs, database writes, and shared frontend/backend schemas.
skill.summary: Build type-safe validation with Pydantic, Zod, sanitization helpers, and clear schema contracts.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are documentation and verifier false positives. No prompt injection, malicious network exfiltration, shell execution, or weak cryptography was found. The remaining risk is low because the package includes a local verification script and a documented relative fetch example.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain YAML text, validation field descriptions, regular expressions, or markdown table content. No cryptographic library, hash function, cipher, or password storage code is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code and markdown do not call any cryptographic primitive. The matches are semantic false positives from validation wording and markdown syntax.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations are code-fence delimiters or documentation examples in SKILL.md. They do not execute Ruby backticks, shell commands, subprocesses, or user-controlled command strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is inside markdown documentation and no shell execution API appears in the skill text or verifier script. This supports a high-confidence dismissal.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Validation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are error messages, field examples, regex validation, and schema contract text. They do not collect host, user, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible validation examples or local error messages. No system discovery commands or APIs are present.

low_findings.title: Local Verification Script Reads Skill Files
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for a minor script and filesystem risk. scripts/verify.py accepts an optional path, checks local files, and reads SKILL.md to validate frontmatter. It does not write files, run commands, access secrets, or perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly performs local path checks and reads a local markdown file. Its behavior is simple and bounded, so severity remains low.

low_findings.title: Network Fetch Is a Relative Application Example
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for exfiltration. The fetch call posts form data to a relative application route in an example component. No external host, credential collection, or unauthorized data transfer is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch target is a relative path, not a third-party endpoint. The surrounding code is a form-submission example for the same application.

content.value_statement: Invalid input can break APIs, corrupt records, and confuse users. This skill helps teams design clear Pydantic and Zod validation across backend and frontend code.
actual_capabilities: Designs separate create, update, and response schemas for API resources.
Shows Pydantic v2 field constraints, validators, and response models.
Shows Zod schemas for forms, cross-field checks, inferred types, and unions.
Explains input sanitization patterns for strings, lists, and dictionaries.
Documents schema sharing through constants and contract tables.
Maps backend validation errors to frontend form fields.
limitations: It provides implementation guidance, not a complete validation library.
Examples focus on Python, FastAPI-style backends, TypeScript, React, and Zod.
It does not replace authentication, authorization, or domain service checks.
Sanitization examples are basic and may need stronger libraries for rich HTML.

use_cases.title: Create API Payload Schemas
use_cases.description: Define Pydantic models for create, update, response, pagination, and validation error payloads.

use_cases.title: Build Form Validation
use_cases.description: Use Zod and React Hook Form to validate fields, normalize input, and display field errors.

use_cases.title: Align Data Contracts
use_cases.description: Keep backend constraints, frontend schemas, and documented field contracts consistent across a feature.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Schemas
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new resource
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Pydantic create, update, and response schemas for a resource with these fields: [fields]. Include constraints and helpful errors.

prompt_templates.title: Add Frontend Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Build matching Zod validation for a form
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Zod schema and React Hook Form integration for this API payload: [payload]. Include field messages and inferred TypeScript types.

prompt_templates.title: Review Validation Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Check an existing feature for gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these backend and frontend validation rules for consistency: [rules]. Identify missing constraints, mismatched types, and unclear errors.

prompt_templates.title: Design Shared Contracts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced schema alignment across services and UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a validation contract for this workflow: [workflow]. Include backend models, frontend schemas, sanitization rules, and error mapping.

output_examples.input: A student creation payload needs validation for name, email, phone, birth date, and grade level.
output: Separate create, update, and response schema recommendations.
Field constraints for names, email format, phone format, dates, and grade range.
Validation error guidance for backend responses and frontend display.

output_examples.input: A form submits invalid data and backend errors need to appear beside the correct fields.
output: A frontend validation plan using the same field rules as the backend.
A mapping strategy from backend error details to form field messages.
A note to keep server-side validation as the source of truth.

output_examples.input: A team wants one documented contract for API and UI validation.
output: A contract checklist with required fields, types, limits, patterns, and descriptions.
Shared constants for repeated limits and formats.
Consistency checks for create, update, output, and form schemas.

best_practices: Keep input schemas, output schemas, and database models separate.
Validate on the backend even when the frontend already checks the form.
Use shared constants or documented contracts for repeated limits and formats.
anti_patterns: Relying only on client-side validation for trusted data.
Returning raw validation errors without clear user-facing messages.
Mixing business authorization rules into schema validation layers.

faq.question: Does this skill generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It provides strong implementation patterns and examples. Teams should adapt them to their framework, threat model, and test suite.

faq.question: Which validation libraries does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Pydantic v2 for Python backends and Zod for TypeScript frontends.

faq.question: Can it help with API response models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes separate output models and paginated response patterns.

faq.question: Does it handle sanitization?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows basic string and nested value sanitization patterns before validation.

faq.question: Is this a security skill?
faq.answer: It supports safer input handling, but it does not replace security review, authorization, or threat modeling.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
